messages.content:
翻译成中文：In Germany, Grand Admiral Hans Georg Von Friedeburg, Karl Dönitz’s successor as head of the Kriegsmarine, and General Hans Kinzel, Chief of Staff of the North- western Armies, turn up at Marshal Montgomery’s headquarters in Lunebrug Heart, south of Hamburg, to negotiate the surrender of three German armies. Montgomery only accepts the capitulation of those fighting in the western area. The armies of the east must surrender to the Soviets.
Karl Dönitz sets up his government’s headquarters in Flensburg. The new chancellor, Count Johann Ludwig (Lutz) Graf Schwerin Von Krosigk will be in charge of making contact with the western Allies to propose a continuation of the war against the Soviet Union.

在德国，作为Kriegsmarine负责人Donitz的继任者的大上将汉斯·乔治·冯·弗里德堡和西北军队参谋长汉斯·金策尔将军来到位于汉堡以南的卢讷堡哈特的蒙哥马利元帅总部，以谈判三支德军的投降事宜。蒙哥马利将只接受在西部地区作战的军队的投降。而东部的军队必须向苏联投降。

卡尔·多尼茨在弗伦斯堡设立了政府总部。新任总理约翰·路德维希（卢茨）·格拉夫·施韦林·冯·克罗西格将负责与西方盟国建立联系，提议继续对抗苏联的战争。

翻译成中文：The Seventh British Army pushes the German troops towards the Kiel Canal. Units from the Third US Army march towards the Austrian city of Linz.
In Czechoslovakia, the Soviets advance northeast of Brno and southeast of Moravska-Ostrava.
In Burma, after 38 months of Japanese occupation, the Allies occupy Rangoon, Syriam and Prome.
In the Pacific theatre, on the Island of Okinawa, the Japanese launch a powerful counterattack with artillery support, while bloody kamikaze attacks against the American fleet sink three destroyers.
In the Philippines, the marines meet fierce opposition on Negros Island. On Mindanao, new landings take place in the Gulf of Davao, near Santa Cruz. On the southeastern tip of Luzon Island, on the Bicol Peninsula, the Japanese still resist.

英国第七军将德军逼向基尔运河。第三美军部队向奥地利的林茨市行军。
在捷克斯洛伐克，苏军向布尔诺市东北部和莫拉夫斯卡-奥斯特拉发市东南部推进。
在缅甸，经过38个月的日本占领，盟军占领仰光、西里亚姆和普罗姆。
在太平洋战区，日本在冲绳岛发动了一次强大的反击，得到了火炮的支持，而血腥的神风特攻队袭击使美国舰队沉没了三艘驱逐舰。
在菲律宾，海军陆战队在尼格罗斯岛遭遇了激烈的抵抗。在棉兰老岛上，新的登陆行动在达沃湾附近的圣克鲁斯进行。在卢森岛东南角的比科尔半岛上，日本人仍在抵抗。

翻译成中文：In Germany, Karl Dönitz and Admiral Hans Georg Von Friedeburg order the immediate end of the submarine war in all seas. However, some commanders follow a slogan that has been spread by word of mouth: “Regenbogen” (Rainbow), which consists in leaving their ports to scuttle their vessels. In the operation, two hundred and thirty two submarines are sunk in the Baltic and the North Sea.
In Marshal Montgomery’s Luneburg Heart headquarters the German delegation signs the surrender of the forces deployed in the Netherlands, the northwest of Germany, Schleswig-Holstein, and Denmark.
In Baldham, Field Marshal Kesselring informs the Allies that he is willing to send members of his staff to Salzburg to negotiate the conditions for capitulation. Eisenhower replies saying that only the surrender of Army Group Centre, South, G, and E will be accepted. A few hours later, the German representatives begin talks with generals Devers, Pacht, and Haislip. In the early evening, the surrender of the troops stationed along the Austrian, Czech, and Swiss borders is announced.

在德国，卡尔·多尼茨和汉斯·乔治·冯·弗里德堡上将下令立即结束所有海域的潜艇战争。然而，一些指挥官听从口耳相传的座右铭：“彩虹”计划，即离开他们的港口以让船只沉没。在这次行动中，有232艘潜艇在波罗的海和北海被击沉。

在蒙哥马利元帅的卢讷堡哈特总部，德国代表团签署了在荷兰、德国西北部、什勒斯维希-荷尔斯泰因和丹麦部署的部队的投降协议。

在巴尔德姆，凯塞尔林元帅告知盟军他愿意派遣他的参谋成员前往萨尔茨堡协商投降条件。艾森豪威尔回复说，只接受对中、南、G和E集团军的投降。几小时后，德国代表与迪弗斯将军、帕赫特将军和海斯利普将军进行了会谈。傍晚初，宣布了驻守在奥地利、捷克和瑞士边境的部队的投降。

翻译成中文：In Innsbruck, Austria, German General Erich Bradenberger and American General Edward Brooks sign the unconditional capitulation of the 19th and the 24th Wehrmacht armies.
In Wageningen (in the Low Countries), in the De Wereld hotel, fifteen kilometers west of Arnhem, the first round of talks take place to discuss the surrender of all the German forces stationed in the Low Countries.
In the United States, during the United Nations conference in San Francisco, the Soviet foreign minister Vyacheslav Molotov tells American Secretary of State Edwar R. Stettinius that the future of Poland has been settled.

在奥地利因斯布鲁克，德国将军埃里希·布拉德伯格和美国将军爱德华·布鲁克斯签署了第19和第24装甲军的无条件投降协议。

在荷兰的瓦赫宁根，位于阿纳姆以西15公里的De Wereld酒店举行了第一轮会谈，讨论在荷兰驻扎的所有德军部队的投降事宜。

在美国旧金山举行的联合国会议期间，苏联外交部长维亚切斯拉夫·莫洛托夫告诉美国国务卿爱德华·斯捷夫尼斯，波兰的未来问题已经解决。

翻译成中文：In Berlin, the bodies of Hitler, Eva Braun, and Goebbels’ family are found by Soviet counterespionage (NKVD) agents near the Third Reich Chancery, and are transferred to Magdeburg.
The Red Army and the Wehrmacht are still fighting in the area between Wismar and Schwerin in East Prussia and the coastal strip between Danzig and Konigsberg.
In Czechoslovakia, the Czech resistance stages the Prague uprising. In the course of the day, two thousand barricades are built throughout the city, and the main bridges over the Vltava River are taken over by the insurgents. Field Marshal Ferdinand Schörner, Commander of Army Group Centre, orders the city garrisons to suppress the rebellion. The SS soldiers that are captured by the partisans are hung from lampposts and burned alive.
In Greece the surrender takes place of the last German garrisons on the islands of Crete, Milo, Leros, Kos, and Piskopi.

在柏林，苏联反情报机构（NKVD）特工在第三帝国总理府附近发现了希特勒、伊娃·布劳恩和戈培尔一家的尸体，并将其转移至马格德堡。

红军和德国国防军仍在东普鲁士的威斯玛尔和施韦林之间的地区以及但泽和科尼斯堡之间的沿海地带进行战斗。

在捷克斯洛伐克，捷克抵抗组织发动了布拉格起义。在一天的时间里，全城建造了两千个路障，并且起义者控制了沃尔塔瓦河上的主要桥梁。中央集团军司令费迪南德·舍尔纳元帅下令城市守军镇压叛乱。被游击队俘虏的纳粹党卫军士兵被吊在路灯杆上活活烧死。

在希腊，最后一批德国守军在克里特岛、米洛岛、勒罗斯岛、科斯岛和皮斯科皮岛投降。

翻译成中文：American troops enter Linz (Austria).
On the Western Front, in Baldham, in southern Germany, all the Wehrmacht forces stationed between the mountains of Bohemia and the Upper Inn, led by Hermann Foertsch, surrender to American General Jacob L. Devers.
Grand Admiral Karl Dönitz requests a four-day window of time to organize the capitulation. General Eisenhower gives him forty-eight hours.

美军部队进入奥地利的林茨市。
在西线，位于德国南部的巴尔德姆，所有驻扎在波希米亚山脉和因河上游之间的德国国防军部队，由赫尔曼·福尔奇指挥，向美国将军雅各布·L·德弗斯投降。

大上将卡尔·多尼茨请求四天的时间来组织投降事宜。艾森豪威尔将军给予他48小时的时间。
翻译成中文：KAPITULIEREN? NEIN
我拒绝投降！
KAPITULIEREN
投降。
NEIN
不。

General Nickhoff, commander of the German forces in Breslau (nowadays Wroclaw, Poland) accepts the conditions for surrender imposed by the Soviets.
In Czechoslovakia, units from the Third US Army occupy the city of Pilsen, while others march towards Prague. General Patton is ordered to stop the advance, because, according to the Allies’ agreements, the country is to be liberated by the Red Army.
Karl Dönitz replies to Eisenhower that the Chief of Operations of the OKW, General Alfred Jodl will fly to Reims to sign the surrender. Additionally, Dönitz announces that Himmler has been discharged from all his government positions, the Schutzstaffel has been completely abolished, and all forms of resistance have been prohibited for SS members. The Germans have tried to delay the capitulation because, for every hour that passes, thousands of civilians and soldiers flee from the Eastern Front towards the lines of the western Allies.

德国军队在弗罗茨瓦夫（现在的波兰城市弗罗茨瓦夫）的指挥官尼克霍夫将军接受苏联方面强加的投降条件。

在捷克斯洛伐克，第三美军的部队占领了比尔森市，其他部队则向布拉格行进。根据盟军的协议，巴顿将军被命令停止前进，因为该国将由红军解放。

卡尔·多尼茨回复艾森豪威尔，德国国防军作战司令部的首席运营官阿尔弗雷德·约德尔将飞往兰斯签署投降协议。此外，多尼茨宣布希姆莱被解除所有政府职务，党卫队已被完全废除，并禁止SS成员进行任何形式的抵抗。德国人试图延迟投降，因为每过一小时，成千上万的平民和士兵从东线逃向西方盟军阵线。

In the Low Countries, in Wageningen, Canadian General Charles Foulkes and Prince Bernard accept the surrender of General Johannes Blaskowitz.
In Burma, the Allies march from Rangoon towards the north to converge with the Indian divisions in Hlegu.
In the Pacific, on the Island of Okinawa, fierce Japanese resistance near the Machinto airfield forces the First Marine Division to slow down its advance. South of Maeda, a regiment from the 77th Division makes headway, while fighting breaks out between the Americans and Japanese units that have infiltrated the Tanabaru sector.

在低地国家的瓦赫宁根，加拿大将军查尔斯·福尔克斯和伯纳德亲王接受了约翰内斯·布拉斯科维茨将军的投降。

在缅甸，盟军从仰光向北行军，以与位于Hlegu的印度师会合。

在太平洋，冲绳岛的马辰岛机场附近剧烈的日军抵抗迫使第一海军师的前进速度减慢。在前进阻力南部，第77师一个团在前进中取得进展，而美军和渗透到田原部门的日军单位之间爆发战斗。
Machinto Airfield
马辰岛机场

General Alfred Jodl flies from Flensburg (Germany) to Reims, in France, to sign before the western Allied commands the surrender of all the German forces that are still fighting.
Initially, Jodl intends to agree to the capitulation only of the forces battling the western Allies, but Eisenhower demands total surrender. General Jodl, after discussing the matter with Dönitz, signs the total and unconditional surrender of all German troops on all fronts. It will take effect on the coming day, the eighth of May.
When the Soviet High Staff is informed that General Jodl has surrendered to the British and the Americans, they consider it a partial capitulation and demands that the German commands surrender to the Red Army.
In France, the German naval base of Lorient and its 25,000 strong garrison, led by General Wilhelm Fahrmbacker, which had been resisting behind enemy lines, gives in.
阿尔弗雷德·约德尔将军从
